timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across Al Jazeera's NATO expansion coverage (per user instruction). https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/3/7/sweden-officially-becomes-32nd-member-of-nato-military-alliance ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/3/13/finnish-leader-says-russia-is-preparing-for-long-conflict-with-the-west ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/4/4/a-year-of-living-less-dangerously-finlands-first-12-months-in-nato . Date approximations per rule 4: April 15, 2022 'analysts said Finland and Sweden would likely apply' — pegged to publication date of AJ analysis piece, since the piece itself dates the assessment. May 16, 2022 'Moscow's full defeat' — analysis piece publication date used as the most defensible date for the framing. Mar 11, 2022 'Finland began considering' — pegged to publication date of AJ piece reporting Finland's initial debate; the actual policy debate began earlier but Mar 11 is the first AJ news anchor in window. June 28, 2022 (Turkey lifts veto at Madrid) — AJ six-months timeline frames this as the day the trilateral memorandum was signed at the Madrid summit; AJ's standalone Erdogan-NATO-summit piece confirms. Source disagreements: AJ framed the May 16 'Moscow's full defeat' moment more triumphally than CNN; both interpretations preserved on their respective timelines per rule. The May 19 'US offers complete backing' entry is AJ's framing; CNN frames the same-week event more transactionally (Biden meeting at White House on May 19, captured separately on CNN's timeline). The June 28-29, 2022 Madrid sequence is captured differently by the two outlets: AJ dates Turkey's veto-lifting to Jun 28 (Madrid trilateral memo signing), while CNN dates NATO's formal invitation to Jun 29; both dates are correct events on consecutive summit days. No private individuals named — all named figures (Erdogan, Biden, Stoltenberg) are public officials.

Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across CNN's NATO expansion coverage (per user instruction). CNN's coverage is unusually well-dated for this subtopic since most articles report a single event with a specific URL date. Contributing articles: https://edition.cnn.com/2022/04/08/politics/finland-sweden-nato-membership ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/05/03/europe/finland-sweden-path-nato-membership-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/05/10/europe/finland-nato-putin-analysis-intl-cmd ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/05/17/politics/biden-sweden-finland ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/05/19/politics/biden-finland-sweden-meeting-white-house ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/05/24/politics/nato-finland-sweden-biden-letter ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/05/27/politics/finland-foreign-minister-interview-dc ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/06/27/politics/nato-turkey-finland-sweden ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/06/28/politics/turkey-finland-sweden-joe-biden-nato ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/06/28/politics/joe-biden-g7-nato ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/06/29/europe/nato-sweden-finland-summit-explainer-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/06/29/politics/joe-biden-nato-day-2 ; https://edition.cnn.com/2022/08/03/politics/senate-vote-nato-finland-sweden ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/02/02/politics/senators-biden-f-16-letter-turkey ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/03/05/europe/turkey-finland-sweden-nato-membership-intl-cmd ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/03/17/world/turkey-finland-nato-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/06/01/europe/nato-turkey-sweden-membership-intl-cmd ; https://edition.cnn.com/2024/03/07/europe/sweden-join-nato-official-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2024/11/18/europe/nato-sweden-finland-wartime-guidance-intl . Date approximations per rule 4: All major event dates correspond to either the CNN URL date (which is the publish date of CNN's day-of coverage) or the well-established event date. June 29, 2022 (Turkey lifts veto, formal NATO invitation) — CNN's piece is dated Jun 29 and frames Turkey's withdrawal of objections as that day's news; the trilateral memorandum was signed Jun 28 (captured by AJ on its timeline), and formal invitation followed Jun 29 (captured here). May 10, 2022 'Putin's nightmare' analysis — pegged to CNN analysis piece publication date since it represents that day's CNN framing. March 17, 2023 (Erdogan signals Finland-only path) — pegged to CNN URL date for Erdogan's announcement that Finland's accession would proceed first. Source disagreements: CNN's coverage emphasised the US diplomatic mechanics (Biden meetings, Senate letters, F-16 linkage) while AJ emphasised the Russia-response and Finland/Sweden-side framing; the two timelines therefore diverge by design even though both cover the same broad period. The June 1, 2023 entry captures CNN's framing of Turkey's continued block 'over Quran-burning protests' which was the proximate stated reason; Turkey's underlying objections (PKK concerns) date back further. No private individuals named — all named figures (Biden, Erdogan) are public officials.
